name: reshape-rig
summary: Migration test rig for the os-reshape execution plan (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-os-reshape-execution-plan.md): golden-master capture/compare (F5), the deterministic eval catalogue (F/C/G/O), fault-injection rigs (G2-G6), the sealed-boot self-containment matrix (S1-S5, RED until P2), concurrency rigs (X1-X3, RED until P3), and the scoped grep-gate. RED-first by design: tests tagged [P1-target]/[P2-target]/[P3-target] are EXPECTED to fail until that phase lands.

admission.note: Candidate. NOT in harness/manifest.json and NOT admitted. Promotion is a human step.

input.description: (none) — a test rig; drives the loop + checks via their CLIs.

writes: apps/_drafts/reshape-rig/golden-master.json (capture --write only); all test writes go to mkdtemp copies or OS_RECORD_DIR-redirected temp dirs
exit: Every eval in the §D2 catalogue has an executable, deterministic pass/fail.
invariants: Never mutates the live repo: fault injection happens only in mkdtemp repo copies; loop runs redirect OS_RECORD_DIR/OS_DB/OS_DROPS.
Golden-master comparison masks only volatility (uuids, timestamps, signal numbers, versions, temp paths) — never semantic content.
A [P<n>-target] test failing before phase n is the expected RED; the same test failing after phase n is a regression.
